methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Lila Sciences is a Series A biotech company founded by Geoffrey von Maltzahn and Molly Gibson, having raised $200M to date. Valuation analysis applies two applicable triangulation methods against a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public peers in Biotech & Health.

Stage-progression modeling uses the $200M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a $400M valuation. Peer-median anchoring examines 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B, Seed), Neko Health ($1.8B, Series B), New Limit ($810M, Series B), and Asimov ($800M, Series B)—yielding a median of $810M with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring was not applied; while 3 public Biotech & Health comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) trade at a 6.1x price-to-sales median, Lila's revenue was not available for calculation.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $340M (low) to $931M (high), with a median implied valuation of $605M. Key uncertainties include stage-specific variability within the peer set—adjacent-stage comparables span near an order of magnitude—and the absence of revenue metrics that would anchor relative valuation. The wide peer range reflects sector dispersion rather than methodological weakness.
